California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
February 2L, l99L
Mr. Glenn A. liti Il-er
P.O. Box l-676
Fair Oaks, CA 95628
Re: Your Request for Informa] Assistance
Our File No. I-91-06 6
Dear I'1r. ltilLer:
This is in response to your letter of January 18, 1991 to
A1ice Hughes of the Cornmission's Technical Assistance and Analysis
oivision, requesting advice concerning your responsibil ities under
the conf I ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Pol-itical Reform Act
(the I'Acttt).1 Because no specific governmental decision is
pending this advice is infornal only.z
OUESTION
You have inquired whether your enployment with the Fair oaks
water District (the district) 
would create a conflict of interest
if you rrere elected to the position of district director.
CONCLUS ION
The Act does not prohibit you fron seekinq election to the
position of director for the Fair Oaks Water District. 
Ho$/ever,
L"cau=" other Iegal provisions could affect your situation, you
should contact the district's 
attorney or the state Attorney
ceneral's office as to those matters, as discussed in this letter.
FACTS
You are conternplating seeking the elective office of director
of the Fair oaks water District. 
You have been an employee of the
district for eighteen years. Until. recently, you held the
position of director of the San Juan Suburban water District.
1 Go,r".n*"nt code sections 81ooo-91-01,5.
are to the Government code.
2 
l.,for*al assistance does not provide
innunity provided by an opinion or formaL
(section 83114; Regulation 18329 (c) . )
A11 statutory references
the requestor with the
written advice.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO a P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 95804-0807 . {916)322-5660


our File No. I -9l.-066
Page 2
ANALY S I S
Every employee of a 1oca1 government agency is considered a
"public oificial" 
for purposes of the Act. 
(Section 82048.)
uirder the facts in this instance, by definition this includes your
present employment lrith the district 
and would also include your
Lonternplat-d position as an elected district 
board member. A
public- officiil 
is prohi.bited from naking, participating in, or
using his or her official 
position to influence a governmental
deciiion in which the official 
is financially interested.
(Section 87100. )
Although public officials 
must generally refrain from
participating in aecisions whi,ch will have a material financial
Lffect on sources of incorne to then (section 87103(c)), salary
from a federal, state, or Ioca1 government agency is not rrincoherl
under the Act. 
(Section 82030(b)(2).) Thus, neither your present
salary as a district 
employee, nor any salary received as a
district 
board member, would give ri-se to a confLict of interest
under Section 87103(c). Therefore, the Act Iilould not Prevent you
frorn participating in governmental decisions upon election to the
distirct 
board despite your district 
employment.
Your status as a former elected director of the San Juan
Suburban water District is not relevant for purposes of the Act's
application to the guestion you have raised in your letter of
January 18, 1991.
The advice provided by the Fair Political Practices
cornmission is lirnited to the provisions of the Act. whether other
Iegal provisions, such as Government code Section 1090, the
g"i"..i 
doctrine of incompatible offices, or any 1oca1 ordinances
iay have some bearing on your situation are beyond the scope of
th-is letter. 
For advice as to these matters you should contact
either the district's 
counsel or the state Attorney General's
office.
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact ne at (916) 322-5901-
S incere Iy,
Scott Ha■labrin
